reasoning: The market probability of 23% in October 2025 seems inflated, likely driven by confusion over the show's season 27 plot, in which Donald Trump sues the town of South Park. Extensive searches up to the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff confirm that no real-world lawsuit or formal cease and desist has been issued by Trump against the creators of South Park. For the question to resolve YES by December 31, 2025, Trump would not only have to initiate legal action in the next 51 days, but this action would also need to unambiguously result in negative repercussions (penalties, restrictions, loss of revenue or subscribers). Given that Trump hasn't acted yet despite the show openly baiting him since July, and because large media conglomerates rarely make instant restrictive concessions (like pulling episodes) without prolonged legal battles, the window for this sequence of events is basically closed.
